subject: Link DCAE MOD design tool to Acumos Adapter
commit_message:
Link DCAE MOD design tool to Acumos Adapter

Add import item to global menu with choices of import models, component specs,
and data formats.

Add prefix to acumos adapter API for consistency with rest of DCAE MOD and to
allow adding acumos adapter to k8s/rancher ingress or other reverse proxy
mechanism so it can be accessed by browser javascript in the design tool.

Remove dcae mod hostname mechanism for callbacks from browser javascript since
browser security only allows javascript to call back to the originating host
port.

Clean up indentation only differences between original NiFi code and ONAP
versions.

Remove 3 ONAP java files that are now identical to original NiFi versions.

Change-Id: I23bbd98d5b171d624ef35088cd821aff2918fc1b
Issue-ID: DCAEGEN2-1860
Signed-off-by: Andrew Gauld <agauld@att.com>

Patch Set 1: Verified-1

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-platform-mod-master-designtool-verify-java/11/ : FAILURE

Unable to build due to a non-resolvable parent POM. Search logs for "[FATAL] Non-resolvable parent POM" to find the logs.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

 * groupId is correct and no typos
 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/dcaegen2-platform-mod-master-designtool-verify-java/11/ )

Patch Set 2:

Do the adapter changes warrant a version bump for the image?

Patch Set 2:

At the time this was submitted, no version of the Acumos Adapter had been released, therefore there were no official versions out there that might conflict and therefore no version bump was required.  If an official version of the Acumos Adapter has since been released, with a conflicting version, it is possible that the delay in merging this, may have rendered a version bump necessary.

Patch Set 2:

However, since the CSIT team wants images always be tagged "latest" and the release team wants images to always be tagged with the build timestamp to the second, the version of the image in the pom.xml doesn't actually affect anything.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> However, since the CSIT team wants images always be tagged "latest" and the release team wants images to always be tagged with the build timestamp to the second, the version of the image in the pom.xml doesn't actually affect anything.

1.0.0 was released 2020-03-04.

When this is merged, presumably we will get a timestamped image in the snapshot tree on nexus3. We'll create a release file that points at that timestamped image, and supply an appropriate tag (!== "1.0.0") as part of the release file.

Patch Set 2:

Agree with Jack, this change requires adapter version bump as 1.0.0 is already released
